Nostalgic old carousel with wooden horses. Officially the Michael Friedsam Memorial Carousel according the plaque on the wall. Evidently those who had ride enjoyed it as their faces were smiling.

I wanted a ticket for my 31⁄2-year-old daughter. She tells me I have to pay $4 for her but also for me. I say I don't care, but he tells me it's mandatory. I pay and we enter, we were alone, so my husband asks to enter with my son in the stroller to take pictures but they tell him that he can not be around the rides, even for a photo. Mine and I sit next to each other on two horses, but they come and tell me I can't sit, I have to stand. To me, they're thieves and they should fail. But since it was empty on Christmas Eve it means people are already avoiding this place and doing well.
